title: Bridge Essay: Ongoing Brain Activity and Its Role in Cognition: Dual versus Baseline Models.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Non-fabrication — spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, creative insight, DMN.
**Phase context:** Spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, creative cognition.

**Ongoing Brain Activity and Its Role in Cognition: Dual versus Baseline Models.** — Neuroscientist (2023). DOI: 10.1177/10738584221081752. PMID: 35611670. What is the role of the brain's ongoing activity for cognition? The predominant perspectives associate ongoing brain activity with resting state, the default-mode network (DMN), and internally oriented mentation. This triad is often contrasted with task states, non-DMN brain networks, and externally oriented mentation, together comprising a "dual model" of brain and cognition.
